title: Heaps
description: Array-based min-heap implementation in C — sift up, sift down, insert, and extract-min for priority queue interview problems.

sections.title: Array-based heap structure
sections.explanation: A binary heap is stored as an array where for index `i`: parent is `(i-1)/2`, left child is `2*i+1`, right child is `2*i+2`. A **min-heap** ensures every parent <= its children. The minimum is always at index 0. This is the foundation of priority queues.

exercise.prompt: Given a min-heap array {1, 3, 2, 7, 5}, print the minimum element, then print the parent value of the element at index 3 (value 7) and index 4 (value 5).

validation.message: Min is 1, parent of index 3 is index 1 (value 3), parent of index 4 is index 1 (value 3)
Use index 0 for min, (i-1)/2 for parent index

exercise.hint: Min is at index 0. Parent of index i is (i-1)/2. Parent of 3 is 1, parent of 4 is 1.

sections.title: Sift up (insert)
sections.explanation: To **insert** into a min-heap: place the new element at the end, then **sift up** — repeatedly swap with parent while the element is smaller than its parent. This restores the heap property in O(log n) time.

exercise.prompt: Implement insert with sift_up. Insert values 5, 3, 8, 1, 4 into an empty min-heap. After all inserts, print the minimum (heap[0]) and the heap size.

validation.message: Minimum should be 1, heap has 5 elements
Implement sift_up comparing with parent and swap upward

exercise.hint: Insert at end, sift_up by comparing with parent at (i-1)/2. After inserting 5,3,8,1,4: min is 1.

sections.title: Sift down (extract min)
sections.explanation: To **extract the minimum**: save heap[0], move the last element to index 0, decrease size, then **sift down** — repeatedly swap with the smaller child while larger than a child. This is O(log n).

exercise.prompt: Build a min-heap with {3, 1, 4, 1, 5}. Extract the minimum three times, printing each extracted value.

validation.message: Extract min gives 1, 1, 3 (the three smallest values in order)
Use sift_down after moving last element to root during extract

exercise.hint: After inserting {3,1,4,1,5}, extracting min three times yields 1, 1, 3.
